claimText: AviList is the first-ever unified global checklist of bird species designed to aid effective bird conservation.
evidenceExcerpt: AviList: The first-ever unified global checklist of bird species to aid effective bird conservation.
examUse: Statement-based question on recent environmental initiatives.
distractorHooks: Claiming it is a regional checklist
Claiming it is for mammals or reptiles instead of birds

claimText: AviList replaces the International Ornithological Committee (IOC) and Clements lists.
evidenceExcerpt: It will replace the International Ornithological Committee (IOC) and Clements lists
examUse: Statement-based question on the evolution of global biodiversity databases.
distractorHooks: Claiming it replaces the IUCN Red List
Claiming it works alongside IOC without replacing it

claimText: AviList is a collaborative global effort involving representatives from BirdLife International and the Cornell Lab of Ornithology.
evidenceExcerpt: Collaborative global effort that included representatives from BirdLife International, the Cornell Lab of Ornithology
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question identifying the organizations behind AviList.
distractorHooks: Claiming it is solely an initiative of UNEP
Claiming it is managed exclusively by the WWF

claimText: The AviList global checklist of bird species will be updated annually.
examUse: Statement-based question on the operational features of AviList.
distractorHooks: Claiming it is a static list with no future updates
